title: Simulating the Lunar Eclipse Turquoise Band with Brute-Force AI
summary: During a lunar eclipse, a narrow green-blue band appears at the moon's edge; popular science says it is ozone absorption. But why a narrow band instead of a full ring? Why does it vanish at deepest totality? We start from the simplest white disk, layer in physics, and compute the band — and through repeated failures discover a bigger problem: AI knows physi…
content: During a lunar eclipse, a narrow green-blue band appears at the moon's edge; popular science says it is ozone absorption. But why a narrow band instead of a full ring? Why does it vanish at deepest totality? We start from the simplest white disk, layer in physics, and compute the band — and through repeated failures discover a bigger problem: AI knows physics too well, and will happily lead you into approximations left behind by earlier researchers.
